channel.title: Sayz Lim
channel.description: I write about macOS, HTML, and CSS for people who want to build a static website.

item.title: Writing in the age of AI
item.description: AI didn’t kill writing—it changed it. I stopped blogging when machines got better at words, then came back when I realized ideas come from living, not polishing.

item.title: Integrating ChatGPT into Keyboard Maestro
item.description: Learn how to integrate ChatGPT into your Keyboard Maestro macros to improve your productivity.

item.title: Help me shape my consultation service
item.description: I’ve recently launched a new landing page for my consultation service, where you can book a quick session with me to discuss the tasks you want to automate. Thi

item.title: Designing the MDN sidebar ad unit
item.description: Learn more about my insights from the BuySellAds and MDN partnership on crafting effective ad units for developers.

item.title: Filtering pivot table data with custom formula in Google Sheets
item.description: Learn how to effectively use custom formulas and the REGEXMATCH function in Google Sheets to filter and analyze data with pivot tables.

item.title: Adding mechanical keyboard sound with Keyboard Maestro
item.description: Enhance your typing with mechanical keyboard sounds using Keyboard Maestro for a fun and nostalgic experience.

item.title: Resetting properties with "all" shorthand in CSS
item.description: Learn how the "all" keyword in CSS simplifies universal styling changes across elements, making design tweaks quicker and more efficient.

item.title: Understanding export PATH with ChatGPT
item.description: I spent the weekend asking ChatGPT what each command in the zprofile configuration file does.

item.title: Better YouTube Parental Control
item.description: How to make sure your children watch only the kids-friendly videos on YouTube.

item.title: Learning about JavaScript Object
item.description: Part of process about learning framework-based frontend development is being able to manipulate JavaScript data types and you often need to understand how to ha

item.title: Overpassing states in React
item.description: Some of the lessons I learned from local state management in React as a beginner.

item.title: Archive daily notes by months in Obsidian
item.description: Use Hazel to set up rules that automatically sort older files into subfolder with monthly pattern.

item.title: Carbon Ads Component in Next.js Framework
item.description: Easily add Carbon Ads to your Next.js site with beginner-friendly tutorial. Seamlessly integrate the component and get the ads running in no time.

item.title: Resolve Mackup Not Reading Preferences in macOS Monterey
item.description: Here is a solution for you to fix the problem with Mackup not syncing properly in the macOS Monterey update.

item.title: Fixing Karabiner Elements Issue on macOS Monterey
item.description: The problem is caused by the lack of permission of essential Karabiner Elements binary files to access Dropbox storage.

item.title: How to add a theme selection feature with vanilla JavaScript
item.description: Learn how to let your site visitors to select dark or light theme on your site with this tutorial.

item.title: How to add dark theme with Sass and CSS variables
item.description: Learn how to store your design tokens with Sass and apply dark them with CSS variables.

item.title: Reading CSV as JSON with Alfred Script Filter
item.description: Learn how to use CSV as the source data in Alfred through Script Filter input.

item.title: How to sort CSS properties with Prettier in Sublime Text
item.description: Learn how to install Prettier to order CSS properties automatically in Sublime Text.

item.title: How to create a robust and reliable Keyboard Maestro macro
item.description: I've written my fair share of macros and put together a series of tips to help you efficiently build and debug a macro.

item.title: How to fix EventMachine C extension error in Jekyll
item.description: Here is a simple solution to resolve unable to load the EventMachine C extension; to use the pure-ruby reactor, require 'em/pure_ruby' error

item.title: Getting Started with Obsidian
item.description: Here are some tips to help you set up Obsidian as a knowledge base for your second brain.

item.title: How to Repeat Actions and Macros in Keyboard Maestro
item.description: Learn the best practice to repeat macros in Keyboard Maestro with the repeat action.

item.title: Prioritize Health
item.description: I am able to perform and think more effectively in both life and work when I make decisions that benefit my physical and mental health.

item.title: Better "Quit All" with Keyboard Maestro
item.description: Combine Alfred and Keyboard Maestro to create a shortcut that quits all applications and choose which apps to keep active in the Dock.

item.title: Text Expansion with Keyboard Maestro
item.description: Tips to use Keyboard Maestro as the main tool to expand most frequently used words.

item.title: HubSpot Deployment Shell Script
item.description: Shell script to help you deploy website easily to HubSpot.

item.title: Tips to Maintain a Forked Repository
item.description: I'm sharing some tips to help you maintain repositories from the projects you forked from GitHub.

item.title: How to Fix 'jekyll-autoprefixer' Error Running Ruby 3.0.0
item.description: Here's a fix for Jekyll if you cannot build your project after updating Ruby to the latest version.

item.title: How to Verify 'osxkeychain helper' Personal Access Token
item.description: If you use GitHub for macOS, you want to verify that your personal token access is stored correctly.

item.title: Self-Host Plausible with Amazon EC2 Free Tier
item.description: Learn how you can set up Plausible on Amazon EC2 with this guide.

item.title: Using Feedbin in 2021
item.description: Feedbin is a solid feed reader. You should check it out.

item.title: Better Way to Set Default Application on macOS
item.description: Learn how you can set and manage the default application on macOS with OpenIn.

item.title: How to Build a Jekyll Component
item.description: Take advantage of Jekyll Include to build a component in your project.

item.title: How to Set Default Window Size on Mac with Keyboard Maestro
item.description: Learn the easiest way to set a default window size for your Mac applications with this Keyboard Maestro macro.

item.title: Evernote Device Limit Workaround
item.description: Find out how you can turn the Evernote website into an application to work around the device limit.

item.title: Replacing DragonDrop with Dropover
item.description: Dropover is one of my must-have macOS apps to boost my productivity by acting as a holding bin for me to move files around.

item.title: Building a Plain HTML Form with ConvertKit
item.description: Follow this guide to integrate ConvertKit into your website by building a lightweight HTML form without loading additional scripts.

item.title: Tips for Using Things Efficiently on macOS
item.description: Here are some of my favorite tips to help you manage the tasks efficiently in Things.

item.description: Here's how you can detect and find conflicting keyboard shortcuts on Mac.

item.description: Here's how you can calculate numbers while typing with Keyboard Maestro Macro.

item.title: Why I Switched from Snappy to CleanShot X
item.description: CleanShot X is the best screenshot app for macOS right now. Find out why I switched over from Snappy to CleanShot X in this post.

item.title: Is Setapp Worth It?
item.description: At $9.99 per month, I will show you how to decide whether subscribing to Setapp is worth the money.

item.description: You can replace selected text quickly without using another text editor.

item.title: QLColorCode Replacement on macOS Catalina
item.description: Quick Look extension QLColorCode is not working on macOS Catalina 10.15. Fortunately, someone built a replacement to bring back this feature to macOS.

item.description: Learn the best practices to set up and configure macOS for web development.

item.title: How to Create a Jekyll Site from Scratch
item.description: In this tutorial, you're going to learn how to start a new Jekyll project and manage the dependencies correctly.

item.title: Getting to Know CleanMyMac
item.description: I’ve been using CleanMyMac since 2012. It completes macOS by offering various tools to maintain and review the system status easily from the dashboard.

item.description: Grammarly is a high-quality free grammar checking tool that helps you become a better writer and editor.

item.title: Speed Up Mission Control in macOS Sierra
item.description: The new Mission Control animation on macOS Sierra is slow. Here’s a way to speed up the animation and make it looks faster.

item.description: Karabiner has stopped working on macOS Sierra. I spent a couple of weeks searching for a solution and found Karabiner Elements that perfectly restores the `Hyper` Key back to macOS Sierra.

item.description: The lack of personalization of MacOS QuickLook plugins can bore the hell out of you. Here are some of my favorites QuickLook plugins that you can customize to showcase your style.

item.title: Write a Journal Entry Every Day
item.description: I write a journal entry occasionally, but to show up and write down what you experience every day is a major challenge to people who are new to journaling. What if I run out of ideas to write?

item.title: Several Windows Are Seeking For Attentions
item.description: Focus is not always associated with blindly doing one thing at a time. Full-screen mode is not the answer to distraction. Sometimes you need to work with several windows at once — in a smart way.

item.title: Warn Before Quitting Macro
item.description: Have you ever wished that the OS X active application will warn you when you try to quit it? Here’s how you can build this feature with Keyboard Maestro.

item.description: BitBar Fuzzy Clock tells time in a readable format on Mac menu bar with a slice of countdown timer to show you the number of productive days left in your lifetime.
